Its extracts have been traditionally used in folk medicine for their potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it a valuable resource for treating various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Myrtoleucodendron rhaphiophyllum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
